# Native Config and MCP Lifecycle Evidence

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/nativeconfigmcp -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to enforcement locations](../enforcement-locations.md)

Executable SSOT: [`native-config-and-mcp.riido.json`](native-config-and-mcp.riido.json).

Evidence artifact: `native-config-mcp-evidence`.

Proves native config materialization and MCP temp-file lifecycle rules from source checks instead of hand-written policy prose.

## Implemented Facts

| Fact | Summary | Evidence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| policy owns native hook decision | Claude command-hook audit is the only native hook surface and requires known trust tier plus bundle allow. | `hook-surface`, `hook-evaluator`, `hook-deny-unknown-tier`, `default-allows-claude-hook` |
| policy owns native config file decision | Codex task-scoped config home is typed but not allowed by the default local policy bundle. | `file-surface`, `file-evaluator`, `default-denies-codex-home`, `settings-deny-codex-home` |
| supervisor applies policy before workdir injection | supervisor converts denied Claude hooks to instruction-only and denied Codex config-home to disabled mode. | `supervisor-hook-policy`, `supervisor-hook-instruction-only`, `supervisor-codex-home-policy`, `supervisor-codex-home-disabled`, `supervisor-inject-runtime-config` |
| workdir materializes deterministic native config evidence | workdir records generated files in the native config manifest and writes Claude hook artifacts only when the plan includes them. | `workdir-manifest-generated-files`, `workdir-claude-settings`, `workdir-claude-hook-script`, `workdir-claude-hook-test` |
| Codex config home remains disabled by default | Codex primary instruction file remains AGENTS.md while .codex/config.toml is absent when config home mode is disabled. | `workdir-codex-home-disabled-test`, `workdir-codex-agents-test`, `provider-codex-no-invent-home` |
| MCP config path is a temp-file capability | Claude StartCommand includes strict MCP args only when a path exists and registers that path in TempFiles. | `start-command-temp-files`, `claude-mcp-args`, `claude-mcp-test` |
| session owns MCP temp-file cleanup | session cleanup runs after provider finish and tests cover process exit, cancellation, and timeout cleanup paths. | `session-finish-cleanup`, `session-exit-cleanup-test`, `session-cancel-cleanup-test`, `session-timeout-cleanup-test` |

## Absent Surfaces

| Surface | Scope | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- |
| workdir does not decide policy | `internal/workdir` | C6 materializes the resolved plan; C7/C4 policy decisions stay outside workdir. |
| Codex adapter does not invent task-scoped CODEX_HOME | `internal/provider/codex` | Codex auth/env preservation is allowed, but task-scoped config home materialization is not default behavior. |
| Claude adapter does not inline raw MCP JSON in args | `internal/provider/claude` | provider command receives --mcp-config path and reports temp files, not raw MCP JSON payloads. |

## Assertions

- workdir materializes only a supervisor-resolved native config plan
- Claude audit hooks require policy allow and known trust tier
- Codex task-scoped config home remains disabled by default
- MCP config is passed by path and registered as session-owned temp file
- session cleanup removes MCP temp files after exit, cancellation, and timeout
